note: One row per (policymaker, policy_area, source). Stance is -2..2 per policy-areas.json. All quotes are verbatim excerpts from primary or near-primary sources surfaced via Exa search.

claims.definition_used: States should lead on AI safety when Congress has not acted; federal law should set a floor, not a ceiling.
claims.quote: Unless and until Congress is able to pass [a strong federal AI safety law], California should continue to lead on policies like SB 1047 that foster innovation while also protecting the public.
claims.source_title: Senator Wiener Responds to Speaker Emerita Pelosi's Opposition to SB 1047

claims.definition_used: States are laboratories of democracy; federal preemption would remove California's ability to respond flexibly to AI concerns.
claims.quote: Our work in California demonstrates the vital role states play as laboratories of democracy.
claims.source_title: California lawmakers plead with Congress to protect state AI laws (SF Chronicle)
claims.notes: Co-signed by 20 Democratic state lawmakers, led by Wiener.

claims.definition_used: Federal preemption of state AI laws serves tech industry lobbying interests, not public protection.
claims.quote: Republicans couldn't pass their ten-year ban on state AI regulations, Trump then went around and skirted Congress with his own executive order, trying to ban states from imposing any AI regulations at all.
claims.source_title: Ocasio-Cortez Calls Out Pattern of Corruption Behind Trump and Republican Efforts to Ban States from Regulating AI

claims.definition_used: A national policy should replace the patchwork of state regulations that burden businesses and threaten American innovation leadership.
claims.quote: Remove barriers to AI adoption, prevent needless state over-regulation and allow the AI supply chain to rapidly grow here in the U.S.
claims.source_title: Cruz to push 'light touch' AI regulation (Punchbowl News)
claims.notes: Previewing forthcoming bill at Senate Commerce Committee hearing.

claims.definition_used: State-level AI bills like SB 1047 are well-intentioned but could stifle innovation; federal approach preferred.
claims.quote: Joined other House Members in a letter to Governor Gavin Newsom opposing the bill.
claims.source_title: Pelosi Statement in Opposition to California Senate Bill 1047 (references Khanna's letter)
claims.notes: Opposing a specific state bill is not identical to supporting blanket preemption, but Pelosi frames it as preferring federal approach.

claims.definition_used: As top Democrat on Science, Space and Technology Committee, expressed serious concerns about state-level AI regulation.
claims.quote: Zoe Lofgren, the top Democrat on the Committee of jurisdiction, Science, Space and Technology, has expressed serious concerns to the lead author, Senator Scott Wiener.
claims.source_title: Pelosi Statement in Opposition to California Senate Bill 1047
claims.notes: Pelosi's statement references Eshoo's position. Eshoo is cited but the quote is Pelosi paraphrasing Lofgren.

claims.definition_used: Supports state-level AI regulation in principle but vetoed SB 1047 on grounds it was too broad in scope.
claims.quote: Vetoed SB 1047, stating the bill applied equally to models regardless of whether they posed a serious risk, and lacked an evidence-based approach.
claims.source_title: Governor's SB 1047 Veto Message
claims.notes: Newsom vetoed the specific bill but signed other AI bills; he's not pro-preemption, just rejected this approach.

claims.definition_used: Open release of sophisticated AI model weights raises questions about misuse potential when minimal protections are built in.
claims.quote: Meta's choice to distribute LLaMA in such an unrestrained and permissive manner raises important and complicated questions about when and how it is appropriate to openly release sophisticated AI models.
claims.source_title: Hawley and Blumenthal Demand Answers from Meta, Warn of Misuse After Leak
claims.notes: Joint letter with Blumenthal (D). Key crosspartisan convergence case: R + D jointly concerned about unrestricted open weights.

claims.definition_used: Same framing as Hawley: open release of frontier model weights without safeguards risks misuse.
claims.quote: Meta appears not to have even considered the ethical implication of its public release.
claims.source_title: Hawley and Blumenthal Demand Answers from Meta
claims.notes: Co-authored with Hawley (R). Convergence: Privacy Subcommittee Chair (D) and Ranking Member (R) agree.

claims.definition_used: Compute reporting under EO 14110 constitutes expansive executive overreach that Congress never authorized.
claims.quote: The Biden administration's AI executive actions call for a new regulatory order that protects incumbent operators and discourages innovation with supposedly-optional 'best practices' written by all-knowing bureaucrats.
claims.source_title: Sen. Cruz: Attempts to Regulate the Totality of the Internet Will Hurt US Prosperity, Global Competitiveness

claims.definition_used: Foundation models trained with >10^26 FLOPs or with >10M users should trigger mandatory transparency disclosures.
claims.quote: AI FMTA would help users determine if they should trust the model they are using for certain applications, and help identify limitations on data, potential biases, or misleading results that could lead to harmful results.
claims.source_title: AI Foundation Model Transparency Act (AI FMTA)

claims.definition_used: New data center construction should be halted until Congress passes comprehensive AI safeguards including government review.
claims.quote: We cannot sit back and allow a handful of billionaire Big Tech oligarchs to make decisions that will reshape our economy, our democracy and the future of humanity.
claims.source_title: Sanders, Ocasio-Cortez unveil bill to halt data center construction (The Hill)
claims.notes: Data center moratorium maps to compute governance broadly: controlling the physical infrastructure of frontier training.

claims.definition_used: Big tech companies racing to build AI data centers are jacking up utility costs and seeking endless energy without accountability.
claims.quote: All of this harm has occurred, not in spite of, but because of the absence of federal legislation to regulate AI.
claims.source_title: Ocasio-Cortez and Sanders push bill to impose AI data center moratorium (PBS)

claims.definition_used: AI chip export controls need location-verification technology to prevent smuggling to China.
claims.quote: Introduced the Chip Security Act in the U.S. Senate requiring makers of AI chips to include technology to verify the location of their chips before exporting.
claims.source_title: U.S. lawmakers introduce bill to address AI chip smuggling (Reuters)
claims.notes: Bipartisan: House version co-led by Huizenga (R) and Foster (D).

claims.definition_used: Easing H20 chip exports to China would undercut US advantage in the AI race.
claims.quote: By easing export controls on NVIDIA's H-20 and equivalent chips, the United States has made it easier for PRC researchers such as DeepSeek to build more powerful models that can out-compete the United States.
claims.source_title: Ranking Member Shaheen Presses Commerce Secretary On Trump Administration's Decision to Ease AI Export Controls to China

claims.definition_used: Restricting access to leading-edge chips has been the defining barrier for the PRC's efforts to achieve AI parity.
claims.quote: Restricting access to leading-edge chips has been the defining barrier for the PRC's efforts to achieve AI parity.
claims.source_title: Top Senate Democrats Sound Alarm on Administration's Decision to Resume AI Chip Sales to China
claims.notes: Co-signed by Schumer, Reed, Coons, Warren.

claims.definition_used: Advanced chip exports to China are a national security risk.
claims.quote: Co-signed letter expressing 'grave concern' over the Trump administration's decision to reverse course and allow U.S. companies to sell certain advanced semiconductors to the PRC.
claims.source_title: Top Senate Democrats Sound Alarm on Administration's Decision to Resume AI Chip Sales to China

claims.definition_used: H200 chips are vastly more capable than anything China can make; exporting them would squander America's primary advantage.
claims.quote: The Trump administration's announcement that it will allow the export of advanced H200 AI chips to China is a colossal economic and national security failure.
claims.source_title: National Security Democrats Statement on Trump's Decision to Allow Export of Advanced NVIDIA H200 AI Chips to China
claims.notes: Joint statement with Shaheen, Coons, Reed, Schatz, Andy Kim, Bennet, Slotkin.

claims.definition_used: Access to advanced chips would give China's military transformational technology.
claims.quote: Access to these chips would give China's military transformational technology to make its weapons more lethal, carry out more effective cyberattacks against American businesses and critical infrastructure.
claims.source_title: National Security Democrats Statement on H200 AI Chips to China

claims.definition_used: Export controls must close affiliate loopholes and use country-wide controls on advanced chipmaking tools.
claims.quote: I'm working on legislation that would basically set a statutory standard that would ban any affiliate that is 50% or more owned by a blocked entity.
claims.source_title: McCaul Remarks at Hearing on Semiconductor Export Controls
claims.notes: McCaul (R) has been a consistent champion of tighter export controls.

claims.definition_used: AI chip location verification is needed to prevent export control circumvention.
claims.quote: Co-sponsored the Chip Security Act requiring location verification for AI chips before export.
claims.source_title: Chip Security Act co-sponsorship (Reuters)
claims.notes: Bipartisan bill co-led by Huizenga (R), Foster (D), Moolenaar (R), Krishnamoorthi (D).

claims.definition_used: Same: chip location verification to prevent smuggling.
claims.quote: Co-sponsored the Chip Security Act.
claims.source_title: Chip Security Act co-sponsorship (Reuters)

claims.definition_used: AISI should provide a dedicated platform for safety testing, with mandatory third-party audits for bias, safety, and national security risk.
claims.quote: The USAISI will provide a dedicated platform for research, collaboration, and implementation of safety measures in AI development.
claims.source_title: Cantwell, Colleagues Lead Effort to Establish U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute
claims.notes: Bipartisan: co-led with Young (R), Heinrich (D), Rounds (R). Key crosspartisan convergence.

claims.definition_used: AISI work is critical to ensuring AI technologies are developed safely and reflect democratic values.
claims.quote: USAISI's work will be critical to ensuring AI technologies are developed in a manner that reflects our shared democratic values and supports American industry.
claims.source_title: Cantwell, Colleagues Lead Effort to Establish U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute
claims.notes: Todd Young (R-IN) co-leading with Cantwell (D) is a strong crosspartisan signal.

claims.definition_used: Same joint statement on AISI.
claims.quote: With these advancements comes potential challenges and risks that must be addressed in a safe manner.
claims.source_title: Cantwell, Colleagues Lead Effort to Establish AISI

claims.definition_used: Same joint statement on AISI.
claims.quote: The rapid advancement of AI innovation has brought forth immense possibilities and opportunities. With these advancements comes potential challenges and risks that must be addressed in a safe manner.
claims.source_title: Cantwell, Colleagues Lead Effort to Establish AISI
claims.notes: Rounds (R-SD) co-leading is notable as a mainstream conservative supporting safety infrastructure.

claims.definition_used: Large AI labs should test their models for catastrophic safety risk, including biological, cyber, and autonomy risks.
claims.quote: SB 1047 is a highly reasonable bill that asks large AI labs to do what they've already committed to doing, namely, test their large models for catastrophic safety risk.
claims.source_title: Senator Wiener Responds to OpenAI Opposition to SB 1047

claims.definition_used: AI systems are products; developers should face the same liability standards as cars, toys, or pharmaceuticals.
claims.quote: When a defective toy car hurts a child, parents can sue the maker. Why should AI be treated any differently?
claims.source_title: Durbin, Hawley Introduce Bill Allowing Victims to Sue AI Companies (AI LEAD Act)
claims.notes: Key crosspartisan convergence: Durbin (D) + Hawley (R) introduce strict AI product liability together.

claims.definition_used: AI systems should be classified as products under federal product liability law.
claims.quote: The AI LEAD Act classifies AI systems as products and creates a federal cause of action for products liability claims to be brought when an AI system causes harm.
claims.source_title: AI LEAD Act (Durbin-Hawley)
claims.notes: Hawley (R populist) agrees with Durbin (D progressive) on strict liability. The opposite of Lummis (R libertarian).

claims.stance_label: strongly oppose strict liability / support safe harbor
claims.definition_used: AI developers should receive conditional immunity from civil liability if they meet transparency/disclosure requirements.
claims.quote: This legislation doesn't create blanket immunity for AI -- in fact, it requires AI developers to publicly disclose model specifications so professionals can make informed decisions about the AI tools they choose to utilize.
claims.source_title: Lummis Introduces RISE Act to Foster AI Development, Strengthen Professional Responsibility
claims.notes: RISE Act is the opposite of AI LEAD Act. Lummis (R libertarian) vs Hawley (R populist) shows within-party divergence.

claims.definition_used: AI legislation should be incremental and targeted at specific harms, not sweeping regulatory frameworks.
claims.quote: To avoid the U.S. losing this race with China before it has hardly begun, Congress should ensure AI legislation is incremental and targeted.
claims.source_title: Sen. Cruz: Congress Should Not Allow a Big Govt-Big Tech Takeover of AI
claims.notes: No specific liability bill cited, but his overall framework opposes broad regulatory/liability regimes.

claims.stance_label: support governance framework with innovation first
claims.definition_used: SAFE Innovation Framework: Security, Accountability, Foundations, Explainability, with Innovation as North Star.
claims.quote: Innovation must be our North Star. AI could be our most spectacular innovation yet, could lead to generations of prosperity, so Congress must promote its growth here in the United States.
claims.source_title: Schumer Floor Remarks On Launching The SAFE Innovation Framework For AI
claims.notes: Schumer's framework is centrist: pro-regulation but explicitly innovation-first. Roadmap encouraged $32B in nondefense AI innovation but was light on specific regulatory calls.

claims.definition_used: H200 exports would squander America's primary advantage in the AI race.
claims.quote: Co-signed statement calling Trump administration's decision to allow H200 exports 'a colossal economic and national security failure.'
claims.source_title: National Security Democrats Statement on H200 AI Chips to China

claims.definition_used: Same joint statement opposing H200 exports.
claims.quote: Co-signed national security Democrats statement opposing H200 chip exports to China.
claims.source_title: National Security Democrats Statement on H200 AI Chips to China
